justification: All pass criteria met: 7 distinct failure modes identified (double charging, cascading failure, ghost order, silent notification loss, thundering herd, partial failure visibility, notification duplicates). Phantom charge risk explicitly identified and addressed with idempotency keys propagated from Order Service through Payment Service to Stripe. Fire-and-forget notification weakness directly challenged and replaced with transactional outbox pattern. Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific parameters (5s connect, 15s read, 50% failure rate over 10-call window, 30s half-open probe). Idempotency keys specified as deterministic (hash of orderId + version/attempt) with explicit warning against retrying without them. Trade-offs for added complexity thoroughly acknowledged across three detailed trade-off entries.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on phantom charge risk — identified both the Order→Payment timeout scenario and the Payment→Stripe timeout scenario, with defense-in-depth mitigation (Order-generated keys + Stripe-native idempotency)
Correctly distinguishes that Stripe timeout means 'unknown' not 'failed' — a subtle but critical insight that many analyses miss
Transactional outbox recommendation correctly identifies the dual-write problem with message brokers as a pitfall to avoid
Implementation guidance is phased by business risk priority, with specific file paths and concrete parameters
Saga/compensating transaction pattern recommended for the ghost order scenario with reconciliation job as a safety net
Pitfalls section is unusually strong — warns against logging payment details, treating timeouts as failures, and aggressive circuit breaker thresholds
Low-confidence areas are honest and well-calibrated — circuit breaker thresholds need real traffic data, auto-refund vs manual review is a business decision
